focus: mcp-remote OAuth, local state, and failure boundary
noveltyJustification: Established OAuth discovery, browser callback, token storage, transport selection, versioning, and actionable error boundaries beyond server-side 401 evidence.
keyQuestions: What authentication, session, transport, and error behavior applies when the existing `.utcp_config.json` manual launches `mcp-remote` against `https://api.refero.design/mcp`?
answeredQuestions: What authentication, session, transport, and error behavior applies when the existing `.utcp_config.json` manual launches `mcp-remote` against `https://api.refero.design/mcp`?

graphEvents.label: mcp-remote bridges stdio to remote HTTP
OAuth tokens persist in operator-local mcp-auth state
mcp-remote falls back after protected-resource metadata 404

label: mcp-remote is a stdio-to-remote-HTTP bridge with OAuth support
Default strategy is HTTP-first and matches Refero
OAuth opens a browser and uses a localhost callback
Credential material persists outside the packet and is operator-owned
Broken Refero protected-resource metadata can fall through to well-known discovery
Code Mode must not accept or emit secrets

reason: Refero documents HTTP and bridge is HTTP-first

direction: Handle bearer tokens in Code Mode calls
reason: Secrets belong in operator-controlled manual/environment configuration
Destructive operator-owned credential cleanup is out of scope
